_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Fracture condyle Humerus ORIF/ : Fracture condyle Humerus ORIF_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Fracture condyle Humerus ORIF_PPD.CPD .pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists).
procedure_name: ORIF with screw of proximal humerus
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years of experience
clinical_indications: Comminuted or displaced proximal humerus fractures
Proximal humerus fractures where bone would not heal correctly with casting or splinting alone

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure.
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)- confirming the diagnosis

ppd_preauth.q: Were the clinical notes and x-ray report submitted are indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Were the clinical notes and x-ray report submitted are indicative of procedure being done?

common_queries: Clinical notes and x-ray report are not indicative of the proposed procedure at pre-authorization or the procedure being done at claim submission.
X-ray is missing, not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right), or does not confirm the diagnosis at pre-authorization.
Post-procedure imaging study (X-ray) is missing at claim submission.
Detailed procedure/operative note is missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Invoice or barcode of the implant is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Open Reduction Internal Fixation (ORIF) of proximal humerus involves the implementation of implants to guide the healing process of a bone, as well as the open reduction, or setting, of the bone.
It involves fixation of screws and/or plates to enable or facilitate healing.
Rigid fixation prevents micro-motion across lines of fracture to enable healing and prevent infection when plates are used.
In a humerus fracture it is important to restore axial alignment and rotation.
Anatomic reduction and internal fixation follow the principle of reducing the numbers of fragments from 4 to 3, 3 to 2 and 2 to 1 starting distally. Alternatively, a bridging technique may be used.
If the fragments are big enough and the fracture planes are suitable, the fragments may be reduced and held with lag screws or cerclage.
